title: Bad Headaches
body: Headaches that keep coming back or get worse over time — especially in the morning or when lying down — and don't go away with normal pain medicine.

title: Vision Problems
body: Blurry or double vision, losing vision on one side, or other changes in how well you can see that you can't explain.

title: Hearing Loss or Ringing
body: Losing hearing in one or both ears, or hearing a ringing or buzzing sound (called tinnitus) that won't stop.

title: Memory or Thinking Problems
body: Trouble focusing, forgetting things more than usual, feeling confused, or noticing changes in how you think or remember.

title: Seizures
body: Having a seizure for the first time — or changes in seizure patterns if you have had them before — with no clear cause.

title: Weakness or Numbness
body: Weakness, numbness, or a tingling feeling in your arms, legs, or face — especially on just one side of your body.
